vocab.meaning: tự lực cánh sinh (thành bại tại thân)
vocab.example: In individual sports, it’s a sink or swim situation where your success depends entirely on your own grit and preparation.

vocab.meaning: một phần nhỏ trong một bộ máy lớn
vocab.example: In team sports, you have to accept being a cog in the machine, playing your specific role to ensure the whole group functions effectively.
Solo athletes have to shoulder the blame alone when things go south, unlike team players who can share the burden of a loss.

vocab.meaning: nắm vững cách thức thực hiện (một kỹ năng/chiến thuật)
vocab.example: Individual sports require you to know the ropes of every technical aspect yourself, as there’s no teammate to cover your weaknesses.

vocab.meaning: cùng hội cùng thuyền (cùng chung hoàn cảnh)
vocab.example: The beauty of team sports is that everyone is in the same boat, fostering a sense of solidarity that you just don't get in solo disciplines.

vocab.word: keep one's eye on the ball
vocab.meaning: duy trì sự tập trung cao độ
vocab.example: While both require focus, a marathon runner must keep their eye on the ball for hours of solitary effort without any external motivation.

vocab.meaning: làm tròn phần việc của mình
vocab.example: In a relay race, every runner must pull their weight, otherwise the entire team's hard work goes down the drain.

vocab.meaning: vượt lên chính mình/xử lý tốt tình huống khó khăn
vocab.example: Individual sports test your ability to rise to the occasion under immense pressure without a captain to guide you.

vocab.meaning: người độc hành (người thích làm việc một mình)
vocab.example: Individual sports often attract the lone wolf types who prefer personal accountability over collective coordination.

vocab.meaning: truyền lại ngọn đuốc (kế thừa/phối hợp)
vocab.example: In team dynamics, learning when to pass the torch or let a teammate take the lead is crucial for the final victory.
